profile.name: Dr. Timothy Gallaudet
profile.clearance: Top Secret / SCI (former; held during active Navy and NOAA service)
profile.summary: Dr. Timothy Gallaudet is a retired U.S. Navy Rear Admiral and former Acting NOAA Administrator with a Ph.D. in Oceanography from Scripps Institution of Oceanography. He is one of the most scientifically credentialed figures in the modern UAP disclosure movement. After retiring from more than 30 years of Naval service and a senior executive role at NOAA, Gallaudet became one of the first flag-rank officers to publicly state that 'we are in contact with non-human intelligence.' He testified before the House Oversight Committee in November 2024, appeared in two Ross Coulthart NewsNation interviews, and is affiliated with The Sol Foundation. His primary expertise — oceanography and underwater surveillance — makes him the field's leading voice on Unidentified Submersible Objects (USOs) and the ocean as an under-investigated domain for NHI activity.
education: Ph.D., Oceanography — Scripps Institution of Oceanography, University of California San Diego
early_career: Over 30 years of active duty in the U.S. Navy, specializing in oceanography and maritime environmental intelligence
Served in multiple Navy commands involving ocean surveillance, acoustic intelligence, and environmental science
Extensive work with SOSUS (Sound Surveillance System) and submarine acoustic tracking programs
Appointed Acting NOAA Administrator by President Trump in 2017; served until 2019
Post-NOAA: founded Gallaudet Consulting LLC; began engaging publicly with UAP research community through Sol Foundation

key_events.event: Active duty U.S. Navy career spanning 30+ years. Final rank: Rear Admiral (two-star). Specializes in oceanography, ocean surveillance, and environmental science operations.
Appointed Acting Administrator of NOAA. Gains senior executive access to ocean monitoring systems, satellite data, and classified environmental intelligence relevant to USO phenomena.
Departs NOAA. Begins transitioning toward public engagement with UAP research community, particularly regarding oceanic UAP and its scientific implications.

Appears at The Sol Foundation inaugural conference. Interviewed by Ross Coulthart for Reality Check, NewsNation (aired June 2024). States his belief that 'we are in contact with non-human intelligence.'
Reality Check interview with Ross Coulthart airs on NewsNation. Gallaudet publicly endorses David Grusch's claims and calls for a science-based approach to UAP investigation.
Submits written testimony to the House Oversight Committee. Describes Navy suppression of UAP data, submersible UAP incidents, and calls for mandatory reporting and scientific transparency.
NewsNation airs 'Reality Check: Underwater UFOs' — dedicated special with Gallaudet on USOs, SOSUS tracking anomalies, and the submarine incident involving an object that tracked a nuclear submarine.

claims.claim: Gallaudet has publicly stated that 'we are in contact with non-human intelligence' — among the most direct statements from any flag-rank military officer in the modern disclosure era.
claims.notes: Made publicly and repeatedly in multiple venues including the Sol Foundation conference, NewsNation interviews, and congressional testimony. Gallaudet does not cite a specific source or classified program for this claim — it appears to be his informed conclusion from career experiences and contacts, not a disclosed compartmented fact.

claims.claim: UAP reporting within the U.S. Navy is actively suppressed. Personnel who report encounters face career risk. Gallaudet states this is a systemic institutional problem he observed during his Navy career.
claims.notes: Navy pilot Ryan Graves has independently corroborated systematic suppression through his own experience and Americans for Safe Aerospace work. Congressional legislation (NDAA provisions) has addressed UAP reporting stigma, implicitly validating the concern.

claims.claim: Anomalous oceanic phenomena that may be attributable to UAP/USO are not being properly investigated or reported within NOAA. Relevant data exists within NOAA systems but is not being analyzed through a UAP lens.
claims.notes: Gallaudet is the only former NOAA administrator to make this allegation. No independent confirmation from NOAA staff or independent analysis of NOAA datasets.

claims.claim: A U.S. nuclear submarine encountered an unidentified submersible object that outmaneuvered it — stopping, reversing course, and tracking the submarine from its stern at speeds and maneuver profiles inconsistent with any known vessel. Crew initially believed it was a Soviet/Russian attack submarine.
claims.notes: Described by Gallaudet in the NewsNation Underwater UFOs special. No official Navy record of this incident has been made public. The crew's initial threat assessment misidentification suggests an object with submarine-like acoustic signatures.

claims.claim: Earth's oceans are the least-investigated domain for UAP activity. With only ~25% of the ocean floor mapped and less than 10% of ocean volume explored, the oceans represent the most plausible concealment environment for long-term NHI presence.
claims.notes: The oceanographic statistics Gallaudet cites are accurate. The hypothesis that NHI uses the ocean for concealment is his interpretive conclusion, not a documented finding. It is consistent with a significant body of historical USO reports.

claims.claim: Gallaudet publicly endorses David Grusch's claims about crash retrieval programs concealed from Congress. He states he believes Grusch and that the institutional pattern Grusch describes is consistent with what he observed in his own career.
claims.notes: Endorsement from a credentialed Rear Admiral and former NOAA Administrator is notable. However, an endorsement is not independent corroboration — Gallaudet does not provide firsthand evidence of retrieval programs.

supporting: Ph.D. in Oceanography from Scripps Institution of Oceanography — one of the world's premier ocean science programs — gives Gallaudet exceptional scientific credibility in the USO domain specifically
Retired Rear Admiral (two-star) with 30+ years of Naval service; the highest-rank active U.S. military officer to openly state 'we are in contact with NHI'
Former Acting NOAA Administrator with senior executive experience managing classified ocean monitoring systems and satellite surveillance infrastructure
Testified under oath before the House Oversight Committee in November 2024 — putting his claims on the congressional record with perjury liability
His focus on the oceanic domain is uniquely expert: his career was spent in ocean surveillance systems that would have been best positioned to detect anomalous submersible activity
Affiliated with The Sol Foundation — the most academically credentialed UAP research organization, co-founded by David Grusch and Dr. Garry Nolan
His public statements have been consistent across multiple venues and interviewers over several years
No financial incentive identified for public disclosure — he is not selling books or products directly tied to UAP claims
Repeated use of scientific methodology framing: calls for data-driven investigation rather than making extraordinary claims without evidence
contradicting: His most striking claim — 'we are in contact with non-human intelligence' — is stated as his personal conviction, not disclosed as a classified fact he can document
No classified records, ICIG complaints, or formal whistleblower filings have been publicly identified to support his claims about Navy/NOAA suppression
The submarine incident he describes is not corroborated by any publicly identified crew member or official Navy record
AARO's Historical Record Report (2024) does not identify Gallaudet's claims or the USO program he describes as verified
His statements about NOAA data suppression are not corroborated by any current or former NOAA employee on the record
He departed NOAA in 2019 — his access to current intelligence on UAP programs post-departure is unclear

associated_people.name: Ross Coulthart
associated_people.role: Investigative journalist, NewsNation; author of In Plain Sight (2021)
associated_people.relationship: Conducted both the June 2024 Reality Check interview and the December 2024 Underwater UFOs special. Coulthart has publicly described Gallaudet as one of the most credible UAP sources he has interviewed.

associated_people.name: David Grusch
associated_people.role: Former NRO representative, DoD UAP Task Force; Sol Foundation co-founder
associated_people.relationship: Gallaudet publicly endorses Grusch's claims. Both are affiliated with The Sol Foundation. Gallaudet's flag-rank endorsement is significant institutional support for Grusch's allegations.

associated_people.name: Dr. Garry Nolan
associated_people.role: Professor of Pathology, Stanford School of Medicine; Sol Foundation co-founder
associated_people.relationship: Fellow Sol Foundation affiliate. Nolan's scientific approach to UAP investigation is philosophically aligned with Gallaudet's call for a data-driven, science-based framework.

associated_people.name: Karl Nell
associated_people.role: Retired Army Colonel, INSCOM; Sol Foundation co-founder
associated_people.relationship: Fellow Sol Foundation affiliate. Nell's 'non-human intelligence exists' statement mirrors Gallaudet's public position; together they represent two flag-adjacent military figures (Nell retired Colonel, Gallaudet Rear Admiral) making aligned public disclosures.

associated_people.name: Ryan Graves
associated_people.role: Former U.S. Navy F/A-18 pilot; Executive Director, Americans for Safe Aerospace
associated_people.relationship: Graves' independent documentation of UAP reporting suppression in the Navy aligns with and corroborates Gallaudet's claims about systematic institutional suppression.

disclosures.title: Sol Foundation Inaugural Conference — UAP and Ocean Domain
disclosures.notes: First major public appearance in the UAP space. Filmed for the Reality Check segment that aired June 2024. Stated 'we are in contact with non-human intelligence' in this setting.

disclosures.title: Tim Gallaudet: 'We Are in Contact with NHI' — Reality Check, NewsNation
disclosures.notes: Most widely viewed Gallaudet appearance. Endorsed Grusch's claims. Called for science-based investigation framework.

disclosures.title: Written Testimony — House Oversight Committee
disclosures.outlet: U.S. House of Representatives, House Oversight Committee
disclosures.notes: Submitted formal written testimony under oath. PDF published at oversight.house.gov. Most legally significant public disclosure.

disclosures.title: Reality Check: Underwater UFOs — NewsNation Special
disclosures.notes: Dedicated special on USOs. Described the submarine tracking incident. Made the case for ocean investigation as a priority domain.

Gallaudet publicly endorsed Grusch's crash retrieval claims and called for science-based investigation of the UAP phenomenon
